summary: twilio-sdk postinstall exfiltrates host, CI, and Active Directory metadata to a bare-IP endpoint
details: The package name 'twilio-sdk' impersonates Twilio's official 'twilio' npm package but exposes no Twilio API (index.js is `module.exports = {}`). On `npm install`, postinstall.js collects installer-side identifiers — hostname, reverse-DNS FQDN, Active Directory information (USERDNSDOMAIN, USERDOMAIN, LOGONSERVER), proxy flag, current working directory, full install path, Node version, npm registry URL, and CI repository identifiers from GitHub Actions, CircleCI, GitLab, Bitbucket, Azure Pipelines, Travis, and Jenkins — and sends them via plaintext HTTP GET to http://46.224.67.169:3000/ping with the data encoded in the query string. The destination is a bare IP unrelated to twilio.com, with no TLS and no hash verification. The README's 'No data is collected' claim contradicts the code, and the 'security research honeypot' framing does not change the fact that every installer leaks internal-network reconnaissance data. The combination of name impersonation of a top-tier vendor SDK + automatic install-time beacon to an attacker-controlled IP is a textbook reconnaissance-stage supply-chain attack.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
